findings: Independent verification supports the identity: Zenaida macroura is a Columbidae dove with a small head, short thin black bill, pinkish legs, pointed wings, long pointed tail, black wing-coverts spots, and black-bordered white outer-tail tips.

All visible plate text is substantively correct. Cornell gives length 23–34 cm and wingspan 45 cm; its sex-specific weight values support the displayed combined 86–170 g (3.0–6.0 oz) range. Audubon independently gives approximately 23–33 cm, 43 cm, and 85–170 g.

The illustrated bird has a single main head and beak, two wings (one folded and one obscured), two legs, and one long pointed tail. Plumage, pinkish breast/legs, dark eye, black bill, spotted coverts, and tail pattern studies align well with the references.

Minor illustration limitations: the bill is somewhat heavy/long and the eye is slightly oversized; the main perched tail does not make the diagnostic white-edged outer feathers as clear as the separate tail study does. These are not material species errors.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Mourning Dove Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
